findings.evidence: `rg -n "References|Widder|S\. Bernstein|Aspects of the Bernstein theorem|BernsteinChafai|hille-yosida" code/TauCeti/Analysis/CompletelyMonotone/BernsteinTheorem.lean` finds none; lines 14-18 only describe the theorem and Chafaï extraction.
findings.fix: Add a references block crediting the roadmap, Widder Ch. IV, Bernstein (1928), Chafaï (2013), and the internal Hille-Yosida/BernsteinChafai source if this proof was adapted from it.
findings.issue: The headline theorem file has no `## References`/adaptation note, despite proving the HBW theorem and its Chafaï extraction from sources named in the PR description.

findings.evidence: `rg -n "References|Widder|Schilling|Bernstein|Chafaï" code/TauCeti/Analysis/CompletelyMonotone/LaplaceRepresentation.lean` only finds theorem/topic names, not bibliographic credit; the module docstring is lines 13-20.
findings.fix: Add a references block, at least pointing to the OneParameterSemigroups roadmap and Widder/standard Bernstein-function sources used for the easy direction and representation predicate.
findings.issue: The Laplace-representation helper file also lacks source credit for the classical Laplace-transform side of the HBW development.

summary: The PR description names central sources for the Hausdorff--Bernstein--Widder theorem and says the Chafaï proof was adapted from an internal Hille-Yosida formalization, but the new files do not carry that credit in their module docs.
